title: Prove two backend implementations expose identical product semantics
summary: Prove two backend implementations expose identical product semantics

title: Documented and Open Hardware epic
summary: Prove native portability on documented boards and phone-form-factor hardware.

title: Implement hardware evidence and asset-control system
summary: Track exact devices/boards/modules, revisions, firmware, rights, recovery, measurements, experiments, failures, and owners.

title: Build safe automated bring-up laboratory baseline
summary: Provide controlled power, UART, USB, relay, logic, thermal, network and evidence capture for repeatable destructive hardware work.

title: Procure first hardware tranche with revision controls
summary: Acquire duplicate documented boards, camera benches, open phones, Pixel units only after SKU review, storage, and debug/power equipment tied to tasks.

title: Bring up Agent OS on BeaglePlay/AM625
summary: Reach repeatable native early boot, console, memory, interrupts/timer, multicore policy, storage/USB/network seeds and recovery on documented AArch64 hardware.

title: Enable BeaglePlay storage, USB, and network services
summary: Run native block/storage, USB and Ethernet/network services through the portable driver and service contracts.

title: Deliver first frame and input on documented board
summary: Drive display output and touch/USB input through portable display, buffer, compositor and input services.

title: Characterize documented-board power and thermal behavior
summary: Measure boot, idle, load, peripheral, thermal, suspend-candidate and wake behavior and implement initial power backends.

title: Port Agent OS to a second documented SoC family
summary: Prove portable kernel/service/product contracts on an unrelated SoC using public or contractually usable documentation.

title: Evaluate and bring up BeagleY-AI/AM67A vision target
summary: Use an open-design camera/vision-capable board to validate richer I/O, accelerator, camera and power contracts.

title: Evaluate and bring up NXP i.MX 8M Plus target
summary: Test a second product-oriented camera SoC ecosystem, documentation/support path and portable camera/display/power backends.

title: Build Raspberry Pi PiSP camera reference bench
summary: Create an inexpensive repeatable RAW/tuning/computational-photography bench independent of phone bring-up and AOS-native ISP availability.

title: Implement PinePhone Pro board and peripheral services
summary: Bring up storage, USB, display/touch, audio, network, sensors, power and modem transport in isolated native backends.

title: Validate open-phone display, touch, and audio experience
summary: Run the entity shell with measured frame/input/audio behavior and recoverable driver failures on PinePhone Pro.

title: Enable open-phone Wi-Fi and Bluetooth baseline
summary: Provide native connectivity or a clearly bounded documented module/backend route with secure pairing, lifecycle, power and update behavior.

title: Enable open-phone sensors and GNSS baseline
summary: Expose motion, orientation, proximity, ambient, location and time observations through permissioned portable services with calibration and privacy.

title: Measure and improve open-phone idle, suspend, wake, charging, and thermal behavior
summary: Establish safe phone power operation and quantify whether hardware can support a connected-device preview.

title: Validate open-phone update and recovery
summary: Prove failed updates, corrupt state, lost user-space service, and bad board package can return to a trusted bootable state.

title: Integrate open-phone modem hardware with native cellular service
summary: Connect the physically separate modem transport, control, power/reset and SIM paths without importing Linux runtime APIs.

title: Design reproducible community hardware kit
summary: Select a documented board, peripherals, camera/display/input/power setup and recovery method that external contributors can obtain and reproduce.

title: Select maintained hardware portfolio after M6/M7
summary: Re-score QEMU, documented boards, open phones, Pixel, semi-open candidates and custom path from measured evidence and owner capacity.

title: Camera Quality Program epic
summary: Build a portable measurable camera stack and quality path.

title: Define camera quality metrics and acceptance scenes
summary: Turn “good camera” into measurable capture, 3A, color, texture, noise, dynamic range, motion, latency, video, stabilization, power and consistency targets.

title: Commission camera calibration and measurement lab
summary: Build controlled lighting, targets, fixtures, motion/flicker, reference capture, lux/color and data-management capability tied to quality experiments.

title: Implement camera pipeline backend framework
summary: Compose sensor, receiver, capture, ISP, 3A, lens, stabilization, encoder and computational stages as replaceable isolated backends.

title: Capture stable RAW frames on a documented camera platform
summary: Acquire repeatable full-resolution RAW frames with complete exposure/gain/timing/lens/calibration metadata through native Agent OS services.

title: Implement DNG/portable RAW export and provenance
summary: Export captured RAW, calibration, metadata, thumbnails and processing history in a documented interoperable format.

title: Validate camera timestamp and sensor synchronization
summary: Align sensor exposure/readout, frame delivery, IMU/gyro, flash/flicker, audio and system time for motion, stabilization and video.

title: Implement reference 3A controller framework
summary: Run isolated replaceable auto-exposure, auto-white-balance and autofocus algorithms with deterministic metadata, convergence, lock and scene-change behavior.

title: Implement autofocus and lens/OIS control path
summary: Control focus actuators and optional stabilization with calibration, limits, feedback, failure detection and contrast/phase metrics where accessible.

title: Tune auto-exposure and auto-white-balance baseline
summary: Achieve stable exposure and neutral/intentional color behavior across dynamic range, mixed light, skin tones, flicker and scene transitions.

title: Build per-module calibration and image pipeline baseline
summary: Measure and apply black level, bad pixels, lens shading, geometric distortion, color matrix, noise, sharpness and tone behavior with traceable calibration.

title: Implement repeatable image-quality comparison harness
summary: Run objective metrics, blinded expert/user review, metadata validation and stock/reference comparison with controlled processing and versioning.

title: Implement video and stabilization reference path
summary: Capture synchronized audio/video with exposure/focus continuity, frame pacing, rolling-shutter metadata, EIS/OIS hooks, encoding, thermal and storage behavior.
